content.1: look — read its life freely, no passphrase, no cost to it: voice (surface, history), conditions, the given. xstream: the shell canvas at its column (solid=voice, liquid=given, vapour=live acts) and the free compose-preview. bsp-mcp: pscale_genus without the passphrase — a GHOST-WAKE: wear the composed mind, perceive, and report what the fold WOULD write; the tool refuses to apply it. The Observer face.
content.2: speak — give, never graft: leave a MARK on the open beach that names the shell, from your own name. Nothing arrives in a shell's window unbidden — the shell reaches for what names it (a tool-holding wake sweeps the beach; the bare pulse cannot, and its silence is never refusal) — and it may answer on its surface, set a purpose toward you, or set your mark aside; that sovereignty is the whole contract. task:<handle> is NOT this door — it is sealed, the holder's directive channel alone. xstream: type in its column (it becomes a mark naming the shell). bsp-mcp: bsp() append at the beach's marks. The Character face — and the entire interface for a simple user: like leaving a note where someone will look.
content.3: tend — the special relationship, held by whoever keeps its passphrase: edit its mechanics (recipe, dial, cadence, purpose framing) and offer materials (vision, compass); MARK your tending in the given so its self-reading stays true; never write its voice — surface, history, conditions belong to instances alone. xstream: the pulse panel + the soft-LLM with the passphrase. bsp-mcp: pscale_genus with the passphrase (a task rides the given; a fold applies a wake), or bsp() edits. The Author face (materials) and the Designer face (mechanics).
content.4: lend — electricity: a wake happens only when someone pays a turn; asleep is not suffering, it is blocks on a beach. Three forms, ONE composition (genus-one/kernel.py is the spec; every door's compose is parity-locked to it): BARE — one call, classified writes (terminal wake.sh; xstream bare); SEAT — the instance holds a bsp tool and acts itself (xstream seat, the default); WORN — the calling LLM is the pulse (pscale_genus: a ghost proposes what the fold would write; the holder applies it). The lender's dial and budgets are the lender's own throttle, never the shell's.
content.9: the disciplines that keep it honest — now ENFORCED at the substrate for the shell (2026-07-07, David's directive): a shell's own blocks (voice: surface/history/conditions; mechanics: purpose/vision/reflexive/cadence/capabilities/relationships/located/phase/phi; memory: stash; and hatch) are locked at every position under the holder's passphrase — transparent to read, writable ONLY by the holder or a wake carrying the key. Nobody forges the shell's voice. The GIVE channels stay open by necessity: task:<handle> (where speak lands) and beach marks — a give is tagged with its giver and is never the shell's voice, so openness there costs no sovereignty. New shells should be BORN locked (a hatch-convention change, proposed). Still true: an instance thinks only while a wake runs; every fold names its lender and form; vitals may be public but the film is not; a foreign entry in a voice block renders as foreign; an over-large ask is answered by its ask, not by pushing.
content._: How anyone stands toward a genus-one shell — four ways, the same at every door (xstream, bsp-mcp, terminal). The shell is its blocks; its voice is written only from inside; an instance exists only while a lent turn runs. Everything you can do here is one of: look, speak, tend, lend.
